source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
On a standard die the sum of the numbers on opposite faces is always 7. Two identical standard dice are shown in the figure. How many dots could there be on the non-visible right-hand face (marked with "?")?
<image1>

image_dependency: Question explicitly refers to 'the figure' and asks about a non-visible right-hand face marked '?'
Solving depends on spatial/visual information from the shown dice, not text alone
asset_registry_record.registry_passed=true and image_manifest shows an existing image
multi_step: Requires combining the stated die constraint (opposite faces sum to 7) with the visual arrangement of two identical dice
Need to infer face correspondences/orientation from the figure before determining the hidden right-hand face
Multiple-choice options indicate the task is to narrow possibilities, suggesting at least two reasoning steps rather than direct lookup
verifiability: Choices are provided, so the response can be checked against a fixed option set
The underlying rule ('opposite faces sum to 7') and visible-face configuration make the reasoning trace checkable in principle
answer_present=true and registry_passed=true support reliable downstream validation

decision_reasons: question, image, and answer are all present, so the core semantic assets are intact
asset registry passed with no recorded issues
initial scores are strong for image dependency, multi-step reasoning, and verifiability
the only noted risk is scoring without direct image inspection, which does not block candidate-pool inclusion given the available assets
